metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test-verification annotation (e.g. 'test-verified via a direct call or spy assertion') to each of AC2, AC3, AC4, and AC5, matching the pattern already used for AC1, AC6, and AC7.
findings.finding: AC2 (no file-impact→referencing-commit precheck) and AC3–AC5 (no bug-reason / no signature gate for idea bugs/stories/epics) have no stated proving command or test-verification label, unlike AC1, AC6, and AC7 which explicitly say 'test-verified'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: '(test-verified, e.g. assert the referencing-commit check function is not called when from-status is `idea`)'. For AC3: '(test-verified, e.g. assert closing an `idea` bug with no Fixed:/Escalated: reason succeeds)'. For AC4/AC5: '(test-verified, e.g. assert close succeeds for an `idea` story/epic with no certified signature present)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the ## What section exactly which variable is used for the `idea` bypass inside `transition_core` (the caller-supplied `current_status` param, confirmed by the ConcurrencyMismatch re-read to equal `actual_status`) and show the precise condition to add to the existing guard lines.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that transition_core skips the bug-close-reason guard and signature gate 'when the from-status is idea', but transition_core's guards are keyed on `ticket_type` from the re-read state — not on the caller-supplied `current_status`. The plan never names which variable the bypass condition is added to (caller-supplied `current_status` param or re-read `actual_status`), creating an ambiguous placement for the processing protocol.
findings.location: ## What — 'In transition_core (txn.py), skip the bug-close-reason guard and the story/epic signature gate when the from-status is idea'; src/rebar/_commands/txn.py transition_core lines 194–221
findings.suggested_fix: Since transition_core already enforces actual_status == current_status via ConcurrencyMismatch before reaching the guards, it is safe to use the caller-supplied current_status. State: 'add `and current_status != "idea"` to the bug-reason guard condition (line 195) and the signature-gate condition (line 212) in transition_core.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the relevant test file path(s) to the Scope section to cover the test-verified ACs (#1, #6, #7).
findings.finding: AC #7 specifies a 'regression test-verified' deliverable for the non-`idea` close path, but the Scope section lists only two source files (`transition_close.py`, `txn.py`) and names no test files. This leaves the test-file deliverable implied by AC #7 without a corresponding Scope entry, making it an uncovered work item in Scope.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the Scope section with a third bullet, e.g.: `tests/test_transition_close.py` (or equivalent) — add/extend tests asserting: (a) completion verifier not called for `idea → closed`; (b) open-children guard fires for `idea` parent with non-closed child; (c) non-`idea` close still invokes the completion verifier.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a single named constant or policy (e.g. `BYPASS_COMPLETION_GATE_STATUSES = {'idea'}`) importable by both `transition_close.py` and `txn.py`, and add a brief comment or CLAUDE.md note explaining why `idea` uniquely exempts from completion/attestation gates.
findings.finding: The 'idea' bypass condition is hardcoded as a literal string comparison in two separate files (transition_close.py and txn.py), with no central constant or policy object, and no ADR/CLAUDE.md update documenting why this status uniquely bypasses completion gates. If a future status (e.g. 'parked', 'draft') needs similar treatment, maintainers must discover and update both callsites independently.
findings.suggested_fix: In a shared constants module (or at the top of transition_close.py, imported into txn.py): `REJECT_DROP_STATUSES = frozenset({'idea'})`. Replace both `current_status == 'idea'` checks with `current_status in REJECT_DROP_STATUSES`. Add a one-paragraph note in CLAUDE.md under 'Ticket Lifecycle' explaining the bypass rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` to Scope and enumerate the two edits needed: (1) add `'idea'` to `_VALID_STATUSES` (line 29), and (2) update the hardcoded error message in `_validate_status` (line 166) to include `'idea'`.
findings.finding: `transition.py` is an unenumerated edit target: `_VALID_STATUSES` (line 29) and the hardcoded error message (line 166) must include `'idea'` for the `idea → closed` transition to reach `close_ticket` at all. Currently `_validate_status` rejects any `current_status` not in `('open', 'in_progress', 'closed', 'blocked')`, so the bypass guard in `close_ticket` is dead code until `transition.py` is updated.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` — add `'idea'` to `_VALID_STATUSES` (line 29) and update the error string in `_validate_status` (line 166) to include `'idea'` in the list of valid statuses.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `"idea"` as a valid status to the status model **as a prerequisite or concurrent scope item**: at minimum `_VALID_STATUSES` in `transition.py`, the `ticket_status` enum in `common.schema.json`, and `TicketStatus` in `types.py` (plus any reducer state-machine or `_read_status` null-filter that gates reachable statuses), so that `_validate_status` does not reject `current_status="idea"` before the bypass guards in `close_ticket` and `transition_core` are ever reached.
findings.finding: The entire bypass mechanism is inoperable as written because `"idea"` is not a recognized status in the codebase. `_validate_status("current_status", current_status)` in `transition_compute` (transition.py line 198) runs before `close_ticket` is ever called and would reject `current_status="idea"` with a CommandError — the bypass guards in `close_ticket` and `transition_core` are therefore unreachable. The status enum in `common.schema.json`, `_VALID_STATUSES` in `transition.py`, and `TicketStatus` in `types.py` all enumerate `["open", "in_progress", "blocked", "closed", "archived", "deleted"]` with no `"idea"` entry. No evidence of `"idea"` exists anywhere in `src/rebar`.
findings.location: ## Scope / ## What — both modified files; also transition.py line 29 (`_VALID_STATUSES`), common.schema.json `ticket_status` enum, types.py `TicketStatus`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a **Prerequisite / Scope** section to the plan: "Add `"idea"` to `_VALID_STATUSES` (transition.py), the `ticket_status` enum (common.schema.json), and `TicketStatus` (types.py). Verify that `_read_status` does not filter out `"idea"` (it currently returns `None` for statuses in `(None, 'error', 'fsck_needed')`, so `"idea"` would pass through — confirm). This prerequisite must land in the same changeset or a prior one."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `idea` to the `ticket_status` enum in `common.schema.json`, regenerate `types.py` (via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`), add `"idea"` to `_VALID_STATUSES` in `transition.py`, and name all three locations in the Scope section.
findings.finding: The plan uses 'idea' as a TicketStatus discriminator throughout (current_status == 'idea' guards in close_ticket and transition_core), but 'idea' is absent from the canonical status enum in common.schema.json, the generated TicketStatus Literal in types.py, and _VALID_STATUSES in transition.py — the plan never specifies adding it.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope — close_ticket() from-status guard; also src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json ticket_status enum; src/rebar/types.py TicketStatus Literal; src/rebar/_commands/transition.py _VALID_STATUSES
findings.suggested_fix: In common.schema.json change the ticket_status enum to ["open", "in_progress", "blocked", "closed", "archived", "deleted", "idea"]; run `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` to regenerate types.py; add "idea" to _VALID_STATUSES in transition.py; add these three files to the ## Scope section.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
